title: Refund reason catalogue
description: Every valid refund reason + its properties + validation bounds + display metadata. The valid_reason rule reads this catalogue. The RefundObserver + RefundApprovalManager use it to gate customer-vs-admin path + always-approve determination.

reasons.description: Customer explicitly asked for the refund. No product/service issue identified. Discretionary refund at merchant option.
reasons.typical_use_case: 'I changed my mind about the annual membership before using it.' Discretionary refund based on tenant's refund policy.

reasons.description: Service not rendered (missed class due to coach absence, cancelled event, facility closure). Customer paid for something that didn't happen.
reasons.typical_use_case: 'The 8am spin class was cancelled 30 min before start, but I already paid.'
reasons.regulator_relevance: consumer protection law — likely mandated refund window in most jurisdictions

reasons.description: Service rendered but quality issue (equipment failure mid-class, coach absence, facility issue during a paid slot). Customer received something substandard.
reasons.typical_use_case: 'The gym A/C failed during my hour-long PT session — the room was 30°C.'
reasons.regulator_relevance: consumer protection law — likely partial refund mandate

reasons.description: Provider or ops caught a duplicate capture (same invoice paid twice due to network retry / customer double-clicking / provider bug). AUTO-APPROVE — system-initiated.
reasons.typical_use_case: Finance ops caught the duplicate via nightly reconciliation. Refund is 100% automated — no human approval needed.
reasons.regulator_relevance: PCI-DSS + card network rules — duplicate charges must be reversed promptly

reasons.description: Fraud team confirmed the card was stolen (chargeback prevention). Admin-only reason. Customer NEVER sees this reason on their notification — the notification presents a generic 'refund processed for security reasons' message.
reasons.typical_use_case: Card processor flagged a transaction as fraudulent; ops confirms via manual review before refunding.
reasons.regulator_relevance: PCI-DSS + AML — fraud confirmation triggers additional audit trail requirements

reasons.description: Membership cancelled mid-period → prorated refund of unused time. Typically used with refund_type='prorated'.
reasons.typical_use_case: 'Cancelling my annual membership 3 months in — refund me for the remaining 9 months.' Prorated refund via ProrationCalculator.
reasons.regulator_relevance: consumer protection law — proration may be regulator-mandated in some jurisdictions

reasons.description: Ops caught a billing error (wrong plan applied, coupon should have been honoured, tax mis-calculated). Admin-only reason. Requires approval.
reasons.typical_use_case: 'Our checkout page had a bug for 2 hours; 12 customers were charged the annual rate instead of monthly. Refund the difference to each.'
reasons.regulator_relevance: US tax law + SOX §404 — billing corrections require audit trail

reasons.description: Customer threatened chargeback; ops issues refund proactively to avoid provider fees + network scrutiny. Often initiated by payment::PaymentDisputeAccepted event.
reasons.typical_use_case: Customer wrote 'I'll charge back if you don't refund' in a support ticket — refunding avoids the ~$25 chargeback fee + network scrutiny.
reasons.regulator_relevance: Card network rules — chargeback rate above 1% triggers processor scrutiny; proactive refunds keep rate low

reasons.description: Data protection authority / consumer protection agency directive (e.g. cooling-off period breach, deceptive practices ruling, GDPR non-compliance findings). Admin-only reason. Legal-team-approved.
reasons.typical_use_case: 'CA regulator ruled our cooling-off period disclosure was unclear. Refund the 47 affected customers.'
reasons.regulator_relevance: GDPR / CCPA / consumer protection law — regulator-mandated retention + reporting

reasons.description: Finance team caught a downstream accounting error requiring reversal (mis-classified revenue, tax-jurisdiction correction, currency-conversion error post-payment). Admin-only reason. Finance-team-approved.
reasons.typical_use_case: 'Q3 audit caught 3 payments incorrectly attributed to CA tax jurisdiction; refund the incorrect tax + re-invoice with correct tax.'
reasons.regulator_relevance: US tax law + SOX §404 + audit trail requirements

notes: customer_allowed=false reasons are refused from POST /refunds/mine (customer path). RefundObserver returns REFUND_ADMIN_REASON_FROM_CUSTOMER 403 on violation.
auto_approve=true reasons (currently just duplicate_charge) force requires_approval=false regardless of amount / staleness thresholds. System-initiated path.
always_require_approval=true reasons force requires_approval=true regardless of thresholds. These are the sensitive-reason set (fraud / admin / regulator / accounting / chargeback_prevention).

The customer-facing rendering NEVER surfaces admin-only reasons directly — the notification presents a generic message ('refund processed', 'security refund', 'billing correction') to protect ongoing investigations.
